- Guided tours, solar system exploration, and thematic trails

## Key facts
- **What**: Scientific and naturalistic event
- When: May 31, 2026, from 14:30 to 18:00
- Where: Scientific Educational Center – EcoPlanetarium, via ai Ronchi, Tradate (VA)
- Who: Organized by Parco Pineta Appiano Gentile
- Cost: Free entry, paid activities only for the EcoPlanetarium

On Sunday, May 31, 2026, from 14:30 to 18:00, the Scientific Educational Center of Tradate opens its doors for a day dedicated to the discovery of nature and space. Located in the heart of Parco Pineta, the center offers a variety of activities for all ages, allowing visitors to explore the solar system, participate in guided tours, and walk along thematic trails.

Among the proposed activities, the journey in the EcoPlanetarium stands out, a unique experience that allows you to explore the solar system. This activity is the only one that requires payment and booking. Additionally, visitors can participate in guided tours of the Astronomical Observatory, bookable on-site, and the explorer's trials, a path of experiences and games for the youngest. The Magic of the Forest Trail and the Nature Trail offer instead an immersive experience in nature, with guided visits or independently.

To participate, visitors can choose between two options: free entry to the center, which includes all activities except the Planetarium, or the complete package for the EcoPlanetarium. Booking is simple: just select the desired type on the official website and add the seats to the cart. At the entrance, there is no need to print anything; just communicate the surname to the staff at the gate.

### Activities and Bookings
The Scientific Educational Center of Tradate offers a day full of activities for all ages. Among the main attractions are:
- Journey in EcoPlanetarium: 'Exploring the solar system' (only paid activity)
- Visit to the Astronomical Observatory (to be booked on-site)
- The explorer's trials (+8 years): experiences and games for the youngest
- Magic of the Forest Trail: an enchanted path suitable for everyone
- Solar System: a walk among the planets
- Nature Trail: guided visit with the GEV or independently

To participate, visitors can choose between two options:
- OPTION A: Free entry to the Center (all included except Planetarium)
- OPTION B: Complete package for the EcoPlanetarium Junior/Teens&Adults

Booking is simple and can be done online on the center's official website. Once the desired type has been selected, just add the seats to the cart. At the entrance, there is no need to print anything; just communicate the surname to the staff at the gate. The event at the Centro Didattico Scientifico in Tradate represents a unique opportunity for families and science and nature enthusiasts to spend a day focused on learning and fun. The activities offered are designed to engage both children and adults, providing educational and entertaining experiences.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and live in Italy, the event offers a chance to spend a day with family, taking advantage of the free and paid activities offered by the center. The proximity of Parco Pineta to the Italian-Swiss border makes the event easily accessible, allowing cross-border workers to participate without having to undertake long journeys.

### Comparison with Similar Events
Compared to other similar events, the event at the Centro Didattico Scientifico in Tradate stands out for the variety of activities offered and the focus on scientific education. While other events might concentrate on a single aspect, such as astronomy or nature, this event provides a comprehensive experience that combines both areas.

### Practical Scenarios
Imagine you are a parent looking for an educational and fun activity for your children. The event at the Centro Didattico Scientifico in Tradate offers a perfect solution. You can choose to participate in guided tours, explore the solar system, or simply enjoy a walk along the Sentiero della Magia del Bosco. The activities are designed to be inclusive and suitable for all ages, making the experience accessible to the whole family.

### Comparative Table
To help you choose the activities most suited to your needs, here is a comparative table of the main activities offered:

| Activity | Description | Recommended Age | Cost |
|----------|-------------|----------------|-------|
| Journey in EcoPlanetarium | Exploration of the solar system | All | Paid |
| Visit Astronomical Observatory | Guided tour of the observatory | All | To be booked on site |
| The Explorer's Tests | Experiences and games for the youngest | +8 years | Free |
| Sentiero della Magia del Bosco | Enchanted path in nature | All | Free |
| Solar System | Walk among the planets | All | Free |
| Nature Trail | Guided tour with GEV or independently | All | Free |

This table helps you better understand the available options and plan your day at the Centro Didattico Scientifico in Tradate.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). To participate in the event at the Centro Didattico Scientifico di Tradate, follow these simple and clear steps:

### Booking Procedure
1. **Choose your preferred option**: Visit the center's official website and select between free entry to the center or the complete package for the EcoPlanetarium.
2. Add seats to your cart: Once you've selected the option, add the desired number of seats to your cart.
3. Provide your last name at the entrance: At the entrance, you won't need to print anything; simply provide your last name to the staff at the gate.
4. Enjoy the event: Once inside, you can enjoy all the activities offered by the center.

### Deadlines and Bookings
Bookings for the EcoPlanetarium must be made in advance, while other activities can be booked on-site. Make sure to check availability and book early to avoid disappointment.

### Useful Tools
To plan your day effectively, you can use the [cost-of-living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate event costs and the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare payment options. Additionally, the [events calendar](nav:calendar) helps you stay updated on similar activities in your area.

### Final CTA
If you're a cross-border worker who lives in Italy and works in Switzerland, take advantage of this opportunity to spend a day with your family. Book now and enjoy an afternoon of science and nature at the Centro Didattico Scientifico di Tradate. Use the [cost-of-living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to plan your day and discover other similar activities in the [events calendar](nav:calendar).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/una-domenica-tra-natura-e-spazio-al-centro-didattico-scientifico-di-tradate/2559340/)*

item.title: Moncucco Group: profits doubled and salaries raised
item.description: The Moncucco Hospital Group closed 2025 with a profit of 5.3 million francs and salary adjustments for employees
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Profit doubled to 5.3 million francs in 2025
- Salary adjustments for employees
- Improvement of working conditions

## Key facts
- **What**: Profit doubled and salary adjustments
- When: Year 2025
- Where: Moncucco Hospital Group, Lugano
- Who: Moncucco Hospital Group
- Amount: 5.3 million francs

The Moncucco Hospital Group closed the year 2025 with a positive balance, doubling its profit compared to the previous year. The net profit reached 5.3 million francs, a significant result that testifies to the financial solidity of the group. In addition to the economic results, 2025 also saw substantial improvements in working conditions for employees, with salary adjustments that reflect the group's commitment to valuing its staff.

### Impact on cross-border workers
The salary improvements and working conditions can have a positive impact on cross-border workers employed by the Moncucco Hospital Group. With the increase in salaries, cross-border workers could see an improvement in their purchasing power, especially considering that the Canton of Ticino offers a lower cost of living compared to other regions of Switzerland.

### Comparison with 2024
In 2024, the Moncucco Hospital Group had recorded a profit of 2.65 million francs. The doubling of the profit in 2025 represents a significant increase, which can be attributed to efficient management and a well-defined corporate strategy. This positive result is a strong signal for workers and the local community, who can count on a solid and growing healthcare institution.

### Future prospects
With the positive results of 2025, the Moncucco Hospital Group looks optimistically to the future. Salary adjustments and improved working conditions are an investment in the quality of the service offered, which in turn benefits both patients and employees. For cross-border workers, this means a more stable and remunerative work environment, with opportunities for professional growth.

### Comparative table

| Year | Net Profit (in million francs) | Salary Adjustments |
|------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| 2024 | 2.65 | No |
| 2025 | 5.3 | Yes | ## Analysis of Implications
The doubling of the Moncucco Hospital Group's profit not only reflects effective financial management but also has significant implications for workers, particularly for cross-border commuters who represent an important part of the workforce in the Canton of Ticino. Salary adjustments and improved working conditions can have a direct impact on the quality of life of cross-border workers, who often have to manage high costs related to commuting and the cost of living in Switzerland.

### Tax Impact
For cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, it is important to consider the tax implications of salary adjustments. With withholding tax, cross-border workers pay taxes in Switzerland but can benefit from bilateral agreements that avoid double taxation. It is crucial for cross-border workers to understand how these adjustments can affect their net income and plan accordingly.

### Comparison with Other Healthcare Facilities
The Moncucco Hospital Group is not the only healthcare facility in Ticino to report positive results. Other clinics and hospitals in the region have also shown economic growth, thanks to efficient management and targeted investments. This positive trend is a sign of vitality for the Ticino healthcare sector, which plays a crucial role in the local economy.

### Future Scenarios
With the increase in salaries and working conditions, cross-border workers might consider the Moncucco Hospital Group as an increasingly attractive employer. This could lead to greater staff stability and a reduction in turnover, benefiting both employees and the organization. Furthermore, improvements in working conditions can attract new talent, further contributing to the group's growth.

### Comparative Table of Working Conditions

| Year | Salary Adjustments | Improvements in Working Conditions |
|------|----------------------|------------------------------------------|
| 2024 | No | Limited |
| 2025 | Yes | Significant |

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ## What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers employed by the Moncucco Hospital Group, it is important to be aware of the new opportunities and changes made. Here are some concrete steps that can be taken to best benefit from these novelties:

1. **Check salary adjustments**: Verify your pay slip to ensure that the salary adjustments have been correctly applied. If in doubt, contact the human resources department.
2. Double taxation planning: Consult an accountant or tax expert to understand the impact of salary adjustments on your net income and taxes to be paid. It is important to avoid double taxation and to make the best use of the bilateral agreements between Italy and Switzerland.
3. Improve working conditions: Take advantage of the new working conditions to improve your quality of life. This can include participating in company wellness programs or requesting work flexibility.
4. Pension planning: Consider the impact of salary adjustments on pension contributions. Consult an expert to understand how to maximize contributions to the LPP (professional pension) and AVS (old-age and survivors' insurance).

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who wish to delve deeper, the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch) website offers a series of useful tools, including:
- [Salary calculator](nav:calculator)
- [Double taxation guide](nav:ristorni)
- [Comparison of working conditions](nav:permit-compare)

These tools can help better understand the practical implications of salary adjustments and improved working conditions, providing clear and detailed guidance.

### Conclusion
The doubling of the profit of the Moncucco Hospital Group and the salary adjustments represent a significant opportunity for cross-border workers working in Ticino. Taking advantage of these novelties can improve the quality of life and offer greater opportunities for professional growth. For further information and assistance, consult the tools available on [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch).

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/luganese/1921888/gruppo-ospedaliero-moncucco-utile-raddoppiato-e-migliorano-le-condizioni-di-lavoro)*

item.title: 2026 Pre-filled Tax Return: Available Now
item.description: From April 30, 2026, the 2026 pre-filled tax return is available for all Italian taxpayers. Here's what's changing.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 2026 pre-filled declaration available from today
- Only available for viewing until May 14
- Corrections possible until May 14

## Key facts
- **What**: 2026 pre-filled declaration
- When: From April 30, 2026
- Where: Italian Revenue Agency website
- Who: Italian Revenue Agency
- Amount: Not specified

From this afternoon, Thursday, April 30, 2026, the 2026 pre-filled declaration is available on the Italian Revenue Agency's website for all Italian taxpayers. The agency specified in a note that this availability was confirmed in reference to some press articles derived from official communications from a trade union and a tax assistance center. Some withholding agents, such as employers or pension entities, in recent weeks have noticed anomalies in the single certifications they prepared and have proceeded to correct and resend them. Therefore, the 2026 pre-filled declaration has already been updated with the new data received.

Until May 14, the 2026 pre-filled declaration will be available only for viewing. In case any withholding agent realizes they have made errors in the single certifications issued, the same withholding agent will have to transmit a corrective certification to the Agency. Interested taxpayers will be informed of the presence of the correction in the tax folder, with a personalized notice visible on the pre-filled declaration web page.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Canton Ticino, the 2026 pre-filled declaration represents an opportunity to simplify the tax return. However, it is important to carefully verify the data entered, especially in relation to withholding taxes and AVS and LPP contributions. In case of errors in the single certifications, withholding agents have until May 14 to transmit corrections to the Italian Revenue Agency.

### Procedure for viewing
To access the 2026 pre-filled declaration, taxpayers must connect to the Italian Revenue Agency's website and access their tax folder. Once logged in, it will be possible to view the pre-filled declaration and verify the data entered. In case of discrepancies, it is possible to contact your withholding agent to request the necessary corrections.

### Deadlines and terms
The 2026 pre-filled declaration will be available only for viewing until May 14, 2026. After this date, taxpayers can proceed with modifying and submitting the declaration. It is essential to respect the deadlines to avoid penalties and ensure the correct declaration of income. ## Practical Analysis
The 2026 pre-filled declaration represents a significant step forward in simplifying tax procedures for Italian taxpayers, including cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Canton Ticino. However, it is important to consider some practical aspects to avoid errors and ensure a correct declaration.

### Data Verification
One of the most critical aspects concerns the verification of the data entered in the pre-filled declaration. Cross-border workers must pay particular attention to data relating to withholding taxes, AVS and LPP contributions, and refunds. In case of errors in the single certifications, the tax withholding agents have until May 14 to transmit the corrections to the Tax Agency. Interested taxpayers will be informed of the presence of the correction in the tax folder, with a personalized notice visible on the pre-filled declaration web page.

### Tax Implications
For cross-border workers, the 2026 pre-filled declaration can significantly simplify the income tax declaration process. However, it is fundamental to verify that all data relating to withholding taxes and social contributions are correct. In case of errors, it is possible to request the necessary corrections from your tax withholding agent.

### Concrete Scenarios
Suppose a cross-border worker who works in Italy and resides in Canton Ticino receives a pre-filled declaration with incorrect data relating to AVS contributions. In this case, the cross-border worker must contact their employer to request a corrective certification. Once the correction is received, the Tax Agency will update the pre-filled declaration with the new data.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the introduction of the pre-filled declaration, taxpayers had to manually complete the income tax declaration, a process that could be complex and prone to errors. With the pre-filled declaration, the process has been simplified, reducing the risk of errors and ensuring greater accuracy of the data.

### Concrete Procedures
To access the 2026 pre-filled declaration, taxpayers must follow these steps:
1. Connect to the Tax Agency website.
2. Access your tax folder.
3. View the pre-filled declaration.
4. Verify the entered data.
5. In case of errors, contact your tax withholding agent to request the necessary corrections.
6. Once the corrections are received, update the pre-filled declaration.
7. Send the declaration within the scheduled deadlines.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ## Action
For cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Canton Ticino, the 2026 pre-filled tax return represents an opportunity to simplify the income tax declaration. However, it is important to follow some concrete procedures to ensure a correct and timely declaration.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Access the tax folder**: Connect to the Agenzia delle Entrate website and access your tax folder using SPID or CIE credentials.
2. View the pre-filled declaration: Once logged in, view the 2026 pre-filled declaration.
3. Verify the data: Carefully check the entered data, paying particular attention to withholding taxes, AVS and LPP contributions, and refunds.
4. Request corrections: In case of errors, contact your tax withholding agent to request the necessary corrections.
5. Update the declaration: Once the corrections are received, update the pre-filled declaration with the new data.
6. Submit the declaration: Submit the declaration by the deadlines.

### Deadlines and terms
The 2026 pre-filled declaration will only be available for viewing until May 14, 2026. After this date, taxpayers can proceed with modifying and submitting the declaration. It is essential to respect the deadlines to avoid penalties and ensure the correct declaration of income.

### Useful tools
To facilitate the income tax declaration process, you can use the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to calculate withholding taxes and social contributions in a simple and accurate way.

### Conclusions
The 2026 pre-filled declaration represents a significant step forward in simplifying tax procedures for Italian taxpayers, including cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Canton Ticino. By following the indicated procedures and using the available tools, it is possible to ensure a correct and timely declaration, avoiding errors and penalties.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/30/leggi-notizia/articolo/730-da-oggi-disponibile-per-tutti-la-dichiarazione-precompilata-2026.html)*

item.title: Separations in Ticino: support network for parents
item.description: FaMo+ celebrates 40 years with a new guide for families going through separation. Network approach and round table in Bellinzona.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- FaMo+ celebrates 40 years with a new guide for separations
- Round table in Bellinzona to coordinate professionals
- "Affectionate" guide available for free online

## Key facts
- **What**: Presentation of separation guide and round table
- When: Recent event, date not specified
- Where: Bellinzona, Canton Ticino
- Who: FaMo+ (formerly Ticino Association of Single-Parent and Reconstituted Families)
- Amount: 40 years of association activity

The Famiglie Monoparentali+ (FaMo+) association celebrated its 40 years of activity with a significant event in Bellinzona. During the celebration, a new "Affectionate Guide" for parents going through separation was presented, a booklet designed to support Ticino families during a delicate moment. The guide, available for free on FaMo+'s website, addresses various aspects of separation, including legal, economic, legal, emotional, and emotional aspects.

The event saw the participation of professionals from different sectors, including social workers, psychologists, lawyers, and judges. The president of FaMo+, Sandra Killer, emphasized the importance of a network approach to address the challenges of separation. "The network is made up of very different professionals: from social workers, to psychologists, to lawyers and judges. For this reason, it is important that the actors have the opportunity to communicate and also understand each other better," explained Killer.

The guide, presented by Valentina Testoni, head of training at FaMo+, uses the metaphor of the sea to describe the journey of a separation. "Embarking on a separation can represent a stormy sea, or in any case, waters to navigate without certainty about where you end up," explained Testoni. The booklet was created to meet the needs of an increasing number of Ticino families facing this situation.

### Network approach and round table

The network approach adopted by FaMo+ is fundamental to addressing the complexities of separation. The round table in Bellinzona allowed professionals from different sectors to coordinate and collaborate more effectively. This approach is particularly important in a context like that of Ticino, where families may face unique challenges related to double taxation and other tax issues.

The "affectionate" guide is a concrete example of how the association is working to support families. The document was created to be non-judgmental and to provide clear and useful information. The metaphor of the sea was chosen to help parents visualize the separation process as a journey, with all its challenges and uncertainties.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, separations can present additional challenges related to double taxation and other tax issues. FaMo+'s guide can be a useful tool for navigating these complexities, providing information on how to address the legal and economic aspects of separation.

The guide is available for free on FaMo+'s website, making it accessible to all families in need. The association continues to work to improve the support offered to single-parent and reconstituted families, with the aim of creating a stronger and more coordinated support network. ## Practical implications for Ticino families

The "affectionate" guide by FaMo+ is a significant step towards supporting families going through separation. The document addresses various aspects of separations, providing clear and useful information on how to tackle legal, economic, and emotional challenges. The metaphor of the sea used in the guide helps parents visualize the separation process as a journey, with all its challenges and uncertainties.

### Legal and fiscal aspects

One of the most complex aspects of separations is managing the legal and fiscal issues. The FaMo+ guide provides information on how to address these challenges, including details on how to handle double taxation and other fiscal questions. For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, this information is particularly relevant.

The guide also includes information on how to handle legal issues related to separation, such as the division of assets and child custody. These aspects can be particularly complex and stressful, and the guide provides valuable support for navigating these challenges.

### Emotional and affectionate support

In addition to legal and economic aspects, the FaMo+ guide also addresses the emotional and affectionate aspects of separations. Separation can be an extremely emotional time for parents and children, and the guide provides tips on how to deal with these emotions in a healthy and constructive way.

The metaphor of the sea used in the guide helps parents visualize the separation process as a journey, with all its challenges and uncertainties. This approach can be particularly useful for helping parents manage the stress and anxiety associated with separation.

### Coordination among professionals

The round table in Bellinzona allowed professionals from different sectors to coordinate and collaborate more effectively. This network approach is fundamental for addressing the complexities of separations and providing comprehensive support to families. The "affectionate" guide is a concrete example of how the association is working to support families, providing clear and useful information on how to tackle the challenges of separations.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to get support

If you are a family going through separation in Ticino, there are several steps you can take to get the support you need. Here is a step-by-step procedure to navigate this process:

1. **Download the 'affectionate' guide**: The guide is available for free on the FaMo+ website. You can download and read it to get information on how to face the challenges of separations.
2. Contact FaMo+: If you need further information or support, you can contact FaMo+ to speak with a professional. The association offers a comprehensive support network, including social workers, psychologists, and lawyers.
3. Attend events and round tables: FaMo+ organizes events and round tables to coordinate professionals and provide support to families. Attending these events can be a useful way to get information and support.
4. Use online resources: In addition to the guide, FaMo+ offers a series of online resources that can be useful for facing the challenges of separations. These resources include articles, videos, and interactive tools.
5. Talk to a tax advisor: If you have questions about the tax aspects of separations, such as double taxation, you can talk to a tax advisor to get personalized advice.

### Useful tools

For further information and support, you can use the following tools:

- [Salary calculator](nav:calculator): To calculate your net salary and better understand your tax situation.
- [Tax guide for cross-border workers](nav:tax-return): To get information on how to manage your taxes in Switzerland and Italy.
- [Psychological support](nav:health): To find a professional who can help you manage the emotions related to separation.

If you need further information or support, do not hesitate to contact FaMo+ or attend their events. The support network offered by the association can be a valuable ally in the separation process.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/le-separazioni-un-approccio-di-rete-e-una-guida-affettuosa-432439)*

item.title: Disappointed by Switzerland: low earners feel abandoned
item.description: A survey reveals that over half of the Swiss population considers the system unfair, with low earners feeling treated worse
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Over half of the Swiss population considers the system unfair
- 33% of respondents find the system "rather unfair"
- 65% of high earners are satisfied, only 27% of low earners

## Key facts
- **What**: Survey on social justice in Switzerland
- When: 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Beobachter and GFS Bern
- Amount: 2000 respondents

A Beobachter survey on "justice" has highlighted the discomfort and discontent of a significant portion of the Swiss population. Over half of those surveyed consider the system "unfair," with particular attention to the costs of rent, health insurance, and AHV. The survey, conducted by the GFS research institute in Bern, interviewed 2,000 people, revealing that 33% find the system "rather unfair" and 18% "not fair at all."

### Inequality and perception of justice

The survey highlighted a growing divide between rich and poor, young and old, women and men. Those earning less than 3,000 francs a month show a satisfaction rate of 27%, while among those earning over 130,000 francs, satisfaction rises to 65%. Political scientist Cloé Jans of GFS Bern stated that "whether it's rent, health insurance premiums, or AHV, the issue of costs is currently overshadowing almost everything."

### Justice and money

81% of those surveyed consider the high costs of legal proceedings and lawyers unfair, indicating a growing perception that justice in Switzerland has become a matter of money. Furthermore, the survey highlighted a different vision between generations: 59% of under 40s consider their lifestyle unsustainable and believe that the elderly live at the expense of the young, while only 26% of over 65s share this opinion.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers employed in Switzerland, these data can have significant implications. The perception of social injustice and the pressure on the cost of living could influence their satisfaction and their decision to continue working in Switzerland. Furthermore, the growing divide between generations could influence future policies regarding cross-border workers, particularly with regard to AHV contributions and pensions.

### Conclusion

The Beobachter survey reveals widespread discontent among the Swiss population, with particular attention to the cost of living and the perception of social injustice. For cross-border workers, it is important to monitor these trends and consider how they might affect their working and living conditions in Switzerland.

[Calculate your net salary](nav:calculator) ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers

The Beobachter survey highlighted a series of concerns that could have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Switzerland. Growing dissatisfaction with the cost of living and the perception of social injustice could influence the decision of many cross-border workers to continue working in the country.

### Cost of living and satisfaction

One of the main factors of dissatisfaction is the cost of living, particularly rents and health insurance premiums. For cross-border workers, who often live in border areas with lower living costs compared to Swiss cities, this financial pressure might be less evident. However, the perception of social injustice could still influence their overall satisfaction.

### Inequality and perception of justice

The survey revealed that those earning less than 3,000 francs per month are significantly less satisfied compared to those earning over 130,000 francs. For cross-border workers, who often earn lower salaries compared to their Swiss colleagues, this perception of inequality could be particularly relevant. Cloé Jans' policy from GFS Bern emphasizes the importance of addressing these issues to improve overall satisfaction.

### Generational differences

The survey also highlighted a significant difference in perceptions between generations. 59% of under 40s believe that their lifestyle is not sustainable and that the elderly live at the expense of the young. This could have implications for younger cross-border workers, who might feel particularly affected by these concerns. On the other hand, those over 65 might have a different view, which could influence their decisions regarding work and retirement.

### Implications for future policies

The concerns highlighted by the survey could influence future policies related to cross-border workers. For example, there might be a greater focus on measures to reduce the cost of living and improve the perception of social justice. Additionally, generational differences could lead to specific policies to support young cross-border workers and address concerns regarding pensions.

[Compare your taxes](nav:tax-return)

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do for cross-border workers

In light of the survey results, cross-border workers employed in Switzerland should consider some concrete actions to address the highlighted concerns. Here are some steps that might be helpful:

### Monitor living costs

Cross-border workers should carefully monitor living costs, particularly rents and health insurance premiums. This can help identify potential areas for savings and improve their financial situation. Additionally, it's important to compare living costs in different Swiss cities to find the most affordable options.

### Evaluate job satisfaction

Job satisfaction is an important factor for overall quality of life. Cross-border workers should evaluate their job satisfaction and consider any changes that could improve their situation. This might include seeking new job opportunities or negotiating better working conditions.

### Plan for the future

Financial planning is essential to address concerns about the sustainability of their lifestyle and pensions. Cross-border workers should consider creating a financial plan that takes into account their future needs, including pension planning. This can include exploring investment opportunities and understanding the pension options available in Switzerland.

### Participate in political discussions

Cross-border workers can play an active role in political discussions regarding their working and living conditions. Participating in interest groups and discussing with political representatives can help influence future policies and improve conditions for cross-border workers. Additionally, it's important to stay informed about the latest news and political developments that could affect their situation.

### Use calculation tools

There are several calculation tools available that can help cross-border workers manage their finances and plan for the future. For example, the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help determine net income after taxes and deductions. Additionally, tax comparison tools can help identify the most affordable options for tax returns.

In conclusion, the Beobachter survey results highlight significant concerns for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. Monitoring living costs, evaluating job satisfaction, planning for the future, participating in political discussions, and using calculation tools can help cross-border workers address these concerns and improve their overall quality of life.

[Calculate your net salary](nav:calculator)

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921654/delusi-dalla-svizzera-chi-guadagna-poco-si-sente-abbandonato)*

item.title: End to health insurers' millions for sports
item.description: The National Council approves Lorenzo Quadri's motion to limit health insurers' sponsorships to major sports clubs.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The National Council approves Lorenzo Quadri's motion
- Health insurers will no longer be able to sponsor major sports clubs
- The motion now goes to the Council of States

## Key facts
- **What**: Amendment to the health insurance law
- When: 29 April 2026
- Where: Bern, Switzerland
- Who: National Council, Lorenzo Quadri (Lega/TI)
- Vote: 117 in favor, 71 against, 4 abstentions

The National Council approved with 117 votes in favor, 71 against, and 4 abstentions the motion by Lorenzo Quadri (Lega/TI) calling for an amendment to the health insurance law. The motion aims to limit health insurers' sponsorship of major sports clubs, allowing only support for small and local initiatives that promote movement. The Council of States will now examine the proposal.

Quadri explained that the fact that health insurers, which offer basic insurance, sponsor major sports clubs while premiums increase, is increasingly criticized. It is not understood why premium money is used for clubs with million-dollar budgets. However, the Federal Council rejects the proposal, arguing that funds destined for sponsorships are part of the administrative costs of the insurers and that it is up to them to decide how much to invest in this form of advertising.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Quadri's motion could have a significant impact on cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy. Many Swiss sports clubs, particularly those at the highest level, receive sponsorships from health insurers. If the motion is definitively approved, these clubs may have to seek other sources of financing, which could influence their ability to offer services and sports activities at affordable prices. Cross-border workers participating in these activities may therefore see an increase in costs or a reduction in sports opportunities.

Furthermore, the motion could indirectly affect the insurance sector, as health insurers may have to reduce their administrative costs. This could lead to a reassessment of their marketing and advertising strategies, with possible repercussions on insurance premiums and services offered.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before Quadri's motion, Swiss health insurers were free to sponsor major sports clubs as part of their marketing strategies. This allowed many clubs to obtain significant funding, which contributed to maintaining their budgets and offering high-quality sports activities. However, with the increase in insurance premiums and growing criticism of the use of these funds for sponsorships, the National Council decided to limit these practices.

Quadri's motion represents a significant change in Swiss insurance policy, as it aims to reduce the administrative expenses of health insurers and ensure that insurance premiums are used more efficiently. This could lead to greater transparency and accountability in the insurance sector, ultimately benefiting consumers, including cross-border workers residing in Ticino. ## Analysis of Practical Implications

The motion approved by the National Council could have several practical implications for cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy. Here are some possible scenarios:

### Scenario 1: Reduction of Sponsorships to Major Clubs

If the motion is definitively approved by the Council of States, health insurers would no longer be able to sponsor major sports clubs. This could lead to a reduction in funding for these clubs, which may have to seek other sources of financing or reduce their activities. Cross-border workers participating in these activities could therefore see an increase in costs or a reduction in sports opportunities.

### Scenario 2: Reorientation of Sponsorships towards Local Initiatives

The motion proposes limiting health insurers' sponsorships to small and local initiatives that promote movement. This could lead to an increase in sports opportunities for cross-border workers living in Ticino, as health insurers could sponsor local and accessible sports activities. However, this could also lead to a reduction in high-level sports opportunities, as major clubs may not be able to obtain sufficient funding.

### Scenario 3: Re-evaluation of Health Insurers' Marketing Strategies

The motion could lead to a re-evaluation of health insurers' marketing strategies, which may have to reduce their administrative costs. This could lead to greater transparency and accountability in the insurance sector, ultimately benefiting consumers. However, it could also lead to a reduction in the services offered by health insurers, as they may have to reduce their investments in advertising and marketing.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before Quadri's motion, Swiss health insurers had the freedom to sponsor major sports clubs as part of their marketing strategies. This allowed many clubs to obtain significant funding, which contributed to maintaining their budgets and offering high-quality sports activities. However, with the increase in insurance premiums and growing criticism of the use of these funds for sponsorships, the National Council decided to limit these practices.

Quadri's motion represents a significant change in Swiss insurance policy, as it aims to reduce the administrative expenses of health insurers and ensure that insurance premiums are used more efficiently. This could lead to greater transparency and accountability in the insurance sector, ultimately benefiting consumers, including cross-border workers residing in Ticino.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy could be affected by Quadri's motion in various ways. If the motion is definitively approved, major sports clubs may have to seek other sources of financing, which could affect their ability to offer services and sports activities at affordable prices. Cross-border workers participating in these activities could therefore see an increase in costs or a reduction in sports opportunities.

Furthermore, the motion could indirectly affect the insurance sector, as health insurers may have to reduce their administrative costs. This could lead to a re-evaluation of their marketing and advertising strategies, with possible repercussions on insurance premiums and the services offered. Cross-border workers could therefore see a change in insurance premiums and the services offered by health insurers.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do now

Cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy should closely monitor the evolution of Quadri's motion. If the motion is definitively approved by the Council of States, some concrete steps may be necessary to adapt to the changes:

1. Monitor communications from health insurers: Health insurers may communicate any changes in their sponsorship strategies and services offered. Cross-border workers should pay attention to these communications to be informed about any changes.
2. **Evaluate local sports opportunities**: If health insurers start sponsoring local initiatives, cross-border workers may have new sports opportunities. They should evaluate these opportunities and participate in activities that promote movement.
3. Review insurance premiums: If health insurers reduce their administrative costs, they may also reduce insurance premiums. Cross-border workers should review their insurance premiums and evaluate any savings.
4. Contact health insurers for more information: If cross-border workers have questions or concerns about Quadri's motion and its implications, they should contact their health insurers for more information.

### Useful Tools

For cross-border workers who want to stay informed and manage their situation as best as possible, Frontaliere Ticino offers a series of useful tools:

- Salary calculator: [Salary calculator](nav:calculator)
- Health insurance premium comparator: [Health insurance premium comparator](nav:health)
- Tax deadline calendar: [Tax deadline calendar](nav:calendar)
- Practical guides: Practical guides

### Conclusion

Quadri's motion represents a significant change in Swiss insurance policy and could have important implications for cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy. It is important to closely monitor the evolution of the motion and adapt to changes to manage one's situation as best as possible. By using the tools offered by Frontaliere Ticino, cross-border workers can stay informed and make informed decisions.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921904/stop-ai-milioni-delle-casse-malati-ai-club-sportivi-il-nazionale-approva-la-mozione-di-quadri)*

item.title: Family Festival in Lugano: sharing and inclusion
item.description: On Sunday, April 26, 2026, the Rotary Club Lugano-Lago and the Amélie Association organized a day of sharing and inclusion for families.

## In brief

- Activities for children, families, and volunteers

- Where: Lugano

item.title: Controlled micro-earthquake in Ticino: a successful test
item.description: Researchers triggered a quake in the Gottardo massif. Shocks recorded but not felt on the surface.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Researchers trigger controlled earthquake in Ticino
- Tremors recorded but not perceptible on the surface
- Hyper-sensitive sensors capture even distant earthquakes

## Key facts
- **What**: Controlled micro-earthquake
- When: Recently completed test
- Where: Gotthard Massif, Ticino
- Who: RWTH Aachen University, ETH Zurich, INGV
- Amount: Not specified

For the first time, researchers have triggered a controlled earthquake in the Gotthard Massif, Ticino. The test was a success, announced today the RWTH Aachen University (in Germany), which participated in the experiment. For several days, researchers from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich (ETH), RWTH Aachen University, and the Italian National Institute of Geophysics and Volcanology (INGV) have injected high-pressure water into a fault zone near the «Bedrettolab», an underground laboratory set up in an old construction gallery of the Furka tunnel.

The goal was to modify, in a controlled manner, the tension between the rocks and thus trigger microseisms. The experiment succeeded: an entire series of small tremors were recorded. These tiny earthquakes were not perceptible on the surface. Hundreds of hyper-sensitive sensors had been placed near the fault. According to RWTH Aachen University, these devices are so sensitive that they recorded the earthquake that occurred on April 20 in Japan directly in the Bedrettolab.

### Incredible results

Thanks to the experiment, researchers have been able to measure an earthquake at its origin and no longer on the surface, as is usually the case. The signals obtained are 'incredible', says Florian Amann, director of the project at the University of Aachen. Now we have unique data on the physics of earthquakes.

### Project 'FEAR'

In the long term, the project, called 'FEAR', an English acronym for 'Fault Activation and Earthquake Rupture', should improve the predictability of earthquakes. Researchers want to better understand what happens before an earthquake occurs. A major earthquake is preceded by thousands of small seismic events whose evolution contains a lot of information, emphasizes Amann.

### Precursory signals

So far, despite decades of research, no reliable precursory signal of an earthquake had been identified. Since it is not possible to wait for an earthquake to occur in the right place, it is artificially triggered, explained Stefan Wiemer of ETH Zurich to Keystone-ATS before the experiment.

### Other experiments planned

These microseisms were followed online by three different teams. 'So many people are involved, we were naturally extremely tense and now this first great success: we still have to realize it', indicated Amann, quoted in the press release. Currently, the water that was injected at high pressure into the fault is draining from the mountain. This concludes the current phase of the experiment. Further tests and the analysis of the enormous amounts of data collected are planned in the coming weeks and months. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The experiment conducted in the Gotthard massif could have significant implications for cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. The ability to predict earthquakes could improve the safety and stability of the region, making it a safer place to live and work. Furthermore, the research could lead to new technologies and methods for natural disaster prevention, which could also be useful for other regions of the world.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this experiment, the ability to predict earthquakes was limited. Researchers could only record earthquakes after they had occurred, which limited their ability to prevent damage. With this new method, researchers can now study earthquakes at their point of origin, which could lead to a better understanding of earthquake physics and more effective disaster prevention methods.

### Future scenarios

If researchers can improve earthquake predictability, it may be possible to develop early warning systems that could save lives and reduce material damage. This could be particularly useful for cross-border workers living in seismically active areas, such as Ticino. Additionally, the research could lead to new technologies for constructing safer and more earthquake-resistant buildings, which could improve the quality of life for everyone living in the region.

### Data analysis

The analysis of the vast amounts of data collected during the experiment could lead to new discoveries about earthquake physics. Researchers may discover new patterns and trends that could be used to improve earthquake predictability. Furthermore, data analysis could lead to a better understanding of the mechanisms that lead to earthquakes, which could be useful for the development of new technologies and methods for disaster prevention.

### International collaboration

The experiment was conducted in collaboration between researchers from various international institutions, including RWTH Aachen University, ETH Zurich, and INGV. This international collaboration could lead to better sharing of knowledge and resources, which could accelerate progress in earthquake research. Additionally, the collaboration could lead to new research and development opportunities for researchers worldwide.

For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, it is important to be aware of the potential implications of this experiment. It is advisable to stay informed about the latest research news and any future developments. Additionally, cross-border workers should be prepared for any seismic emergencies, ensuring they have an emergency plan and know the safety procedures in case of an earthquake.

### Concrete procedures

1. **Stay informed**: Cross-border workers should stay informed about the latest research news and any future developments. This can be done through local media, research institution websites, and local authorities.
2. Emergency plan: Cross-border workers should have an emergency plan in case of an earthquake. This plan should include safety procedures, meeting points, and emergency contacts.
3. Preparation: Cross-border workers should be prepared for any seismic emergencies. This can include creating an emergency kit, knowing safety procedures, and participating in emergency drills.
4. Collaboration: Cross-border workers should collaborate with local authorities and research institutions to stay informed about the latest news and to contribute to the research.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to stay informed about the latest research news and any future developments, there are several useful tools available. These include:

item.title: Bedretto Lab: induced micro-earthquakes to study faults
item.description: Researchers from ETH Zurich induced micro-earthquakes in Val Bedretto to study fault behavior
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Researchers from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich (ETHZ) induced micro-earthquakes in Val Bedretto
- Goal: study the behavior of tectonic faults
- Experiment lasted from April 22 to April 27, 2026

## Key facts
- **What**: Inducing micro-earthquakes to study faults
- When: From April 22 to April 27, 2026
- Where: Val Bedretto, Ticino
- Who: Team from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich (ETHZ) and the University of Münster
- Magnitude: Maximum magnitude reached: 0.14

In the heart of the Alps, under a kilometer and a half of rock, a team from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich (ETHZ) and the University of Münster induced thousands of micro-earthquakes. The experiment began on April 22 and concluded last Saturday. The goal was to generate an earthquake of magnitude 1, but it stopped at 0.14. But what was the purpose? “The aim is to understand how faults move: when an earthquake starts or decides to become larger, when it decides to stop. What are the conditions that cause the Earth to move in a certain way,” Domenico Giardini, professor of seismology at ETHZ and director of the Bedretto Lab, responds to SEIDISERA. The underground laboratory was set up in the old bypass gallery used for the construction of the Furka railway tunnel, known as the Bedretto window. For four days, his team injected water under pressure into some tectonic faults of the Alps. “We,” Giardini specifies, “do not work on faults with known historical seismicity, because those faults should be left alone.” Therefore, faults at a depth of one and a half or two kilometers under the mountains are chosen. The process, explains the expert proposing an analogy, is like moving a bookshelf full of books at home. If it is too heavy, you can do two things: “remove the books,” or “put something under” that makes the bookshelf “slide better.” Water is then injected at high pressure into the fault “in order to make it move.” These are faults that “would have moved anyway” and are observed through sensors. The declared goal was to progressively generate stronger micro-earthquakes until reaching magnitude one. The experiment stopped earlier, but this does not detract from the results being satisfactory. What has been observed, Giardini continues, “is that not just one fault moves, but several do.” These are phenomena that are normally studied on the surface, while here the process is actively carried out at depth. At the moment, “we have reached the objectives we wanted”: that is, the “part of the fault on which we had the sensors, on which we had the injection points, has been moved.” And it has been seen where “new wells will be made to inject water further into the fault and make it move for a second part.” The goal remains that of reaching magnitude one: a seismic intensity imperceptible to those on the surface, but significant for the experiments. “If we are able to produce events of a certain magnitude repeatedly,” it is also possible “to understand how not to produce them. This is relevant because the entire industry working underground, “from tunnel construction to deep geothermal energy, has the problem of limiting induced seismicity.” Therefore, if it is possible to understand how to produce earthquakes of a certain size, it is also possible to understand how not to do so. It is therefore not by chance that the interest around the Bedretto Lab and its experiments is high; also because it is the most important laboratory of this type in the world.

### Implications for industry
The implications of the experiments conducted at the Bedretto Lab are significant for the entire industry operating underground. Understanding how faults move and how micro-earthquakes can be induced is crucial for tunnel construction, mining, and deep geothermal energy. These sectors must address the problem of induced seismicity, i.e., earthquakes caused by human activity. Understanding how to avoid causing larger earthquakes is fundamental for the safety and sustainability of these activities. ### Procedures and Methodologies
The experiment conducted at the Bedretto Lab followed a well-defined procedure. The team of researchers injected high-pressure water into selected tectonic faults at a depth of about one and a half to two kilometers beneath the mountains. This process was monitored through sensors that recorded the movement of the faults. The goal was to generate progressively stronger micro-earthquakes, up to a magnitude of one. However, the experiment stopped at a magnitude of 0.14, but the results obtained are still considered satisfactory.

### Future Scenarios
The results obtained at the Bedretto Lab pave the way for new research and practical application scenarios. Understanding the behavior of faults and induced seismicity can lead to significant developments in the field of deep geothermal energy and tunnel construction. Moreover, the ability to predict and control micro-earthquakes can improve the safety of underground operations and reduce the risks associated with induced seismicity.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before these experiments, the understanding of fault behavior and induced seismicity was limited. Studies primarily focused on surface phenomena, while at the Bedretto Lab, work was actively carried out at depth. This innovative approach allowed for more precise data to be obtained and more accurate models to be developed for predicting and controlling induced earthquakes.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers in the construction or geothermal sector in Ticino can benefit from the results of experiments conducted at the Bedretto Lab. Understanding induced seismicity and tectonic faults can improve the safety and efficiency of underground operations. Additionally, the ability to predict and control micro-earthquakes can reduce risks for workers and local communities.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Fault identification**: Select tectonic faults at a depth of about one and a half or two kilometers below the mountains.
2. Sensor installation: Place sensors to monitor fault movement.
3. Water injection: Inject high-pressure water into the selected faults to induce micro-earthquakes.
4. Monitoring and analysis: Record and analyze the data obtained to understand fault behavior.
5. Practical application: Use the results obtained to improve the safety and efficiency of underground operations.

### Useful tools
To delve deeper into the practical implications of the experiments conducted at the Bedretto Lab, cross-border workers can use tools such as the [seismic risk calculator](nav:calculator) and the [insurance comparator](nav:health). These tools can help assess the risks associated with induced seismicity and find the most suitable solutions for their situation.

### Final CTA
For more information and to use useful tools, visit the [seismic risk calculator](nav:calculator) and the [insurance comparator](nav:health).

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3704436)*

item.title: Lavizzara flood: hazard zones plan approved
item.description: The Ticino State Council has approved the hazard zones plan for Lavizzara after the flood on 29-30 June 2024
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Lavizzara hazard plan approved on April 22
- 7 dead and 1 missing in the 2024 flood
- Technical analysis for future scenarios

## Key facts
- **What**: Hazard zones plan for Lavizzara
- When: April 22, 2025
- Where: Municipality of Lavizzara, Ticino
- Who: Ticino State Council
- Amount: Not specified

On April 22, the Ticino government adopted the Hazard Zones Plan for the Municipality of Lavizzara, concerning the phenomenon of flooding and riverbank erosion of the Peccia and Maggia rivers. The Vallemaggia has returned to normal, but the images of what happened in the night between June 29 and 30, 2024, are still vivid in many people's minds, when a downpour turned the life of an entire community upside down, destroying the territory and causing the death of seven people, while a young man from the valley is still missing.

Immediately after the tragedy in Vallemaggia, there was an extraordinary mobilization to help the community. The authorities moved to prevent a similar situation from recurring. In November 2025, the Department of Territory presented to the population of the Municipality of Lavizzara the hazard maps of the watercourses of Val Lavizzara and Val di Peccia affected by the tragic flood of June 29-30, 2024.

### Technical analysis and future scenarios

To the inhabitants of the Municipality of Lavizzara, the Canton wrote on November 14, 'the results of the technical analyses, historical research, and the numerous on-site inspections carried out throughout this year have been illustrated, which have made it possible to define future hazard scenarios, both for the lateral streams and for the Peccia and Maggia rivers.' The reconstruction of what happened and the hazard maps 'represent a fundamental element for defining the final restoration and reconstruction interventions, whose design is already underway, as well as for adapting the territorial planning to the risk scenarios highlighted and to perfect the Communal Emergency Plan.'

Now this hazard zones plan has been adopted by the State Council, as published in today's Official Gazette, referring to a decision of the executive dated April 22. In detail, the Official Gazette states that the Government has adopted 'the Hazard Zones Plan of the Municipality of Lavizzara, concerning the phenomenon of flooding and riverbank erosion of the Peccia river (Sant'Antonio and Piano di Peccia), the Lavizzara river (Mogno) and the Maggia river (Prato Sornico, Lovàlt and Broglio) and the phenomenon of debris flow of the slope watercourses in the locality of Sant'Antonio (Corona di Corte and Öir), Piano di Peccia (Sovénat and San Carlo), Mogno (Ri di Röd and Mognòla) and Prato-Sornico (Ri Scudau and Bosco di Casa).'

Against the adoption decision, 'the right of appeal to the Cantonal Administrative Court, Lugano, is granted within the term of 30 days. The appeal has no suspensive effect.'

A dramatic summer

The summer of 2024, we remember, was characterized by two violent downpours, which occurred a week apart. Before Vallemaggia, in fact, on the evening of June 21, the bad weather hit the Mesolcina, devastating the territory and causing the death of three people. ### Implications for the Community and the Territory

The approval of the hazard zone plan represents a fundamental step for the safety and planning of Lavizzara's territory. The technical analyses and on-site inspections carried out have made it possible to precisely identify the risk areas and define the necessary interventions to prevent future disasters.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the flood, Vallemaggia did not have a detailed plan of the hazard zones. The tragedy of June 29-30, 2024, highlighted the need for a more robust emergency plan and better territorial planning. The hazard maps now approved represent a significant step forward in managing hydrogeological risk.

### Future Scenarios and Planned Interventions

The approved plan includes restoration and reconstruction interventions, as well as adjustments to territorial planning to adapt to the risk scenarios highlighted. These interventions aim to prevent future floods and protect the local community. The authorities have already started designing the final interventions, which will be implemented in the coming months.

### Community Involvement

The population of Lavizzara has been involved from the beginning in the process of defining the hazard zone plan. The authorities have presented the results of the technical and historical-geological analyses, involving the community in planning the interventions. This participatory approach is fundamental to ensuring that the measures adopted are effective and accepted by the local population.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now: procedures and deadlines

With the approval of the hazard zone plan, it is important that the community of Lavizzara is aware of the measures adopted and the procedures to follow in case of emergency. The authorities have published the details of the plan in the Official Gazette, which include the risk areas and preventive measures.

### Appeal to the Cantonal Administrative Court

An appeal against the decision to adopt the hazard zone plan can be made to the Cantonal Administrative Court of Lugano within a 30-day deadline. The appeal does not have a suspensive effect, meaning that the plan will come into force even in the event of an appeal.

### Next steps and interventions

The authorities have already started the design of the final restoration and reconstruction interventions. These interventions aim to prevent future floods and protect the local community. Territorial planning will be adapted to the risk scenarios highlighted, and the Municipal Emergency Plan will be improved.

### Useful tools

For further information and to plan your actions, consult the [hydrogeological risk calculator](nav:calculator) and the [insurance comparator](nav:health). These tools will help you better understand the risks and take the necessary measures to protect your property and your family.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/alluvione-in-lavizzara-il-consiglio-di-stato-approva-i-piani-delle-zone-di-pericolo-432480)*

item.title: Overtourism in Switzerland: Lucerne and Grindelwald collapsing
item.description: Lucerne surpasses Venice in tourist overnight stays. Initiative to block new hotels and Airbnb restrictions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Lucerne surpasses Venice with 1.5 million overnight stays in 2024
- Initiative to block new hotels in Lucerne
- Restrictions on Airbnb in Ticino, Zurich, Lucerne, and Geneva

## Key facts
- **What**: Initiative to block new hotels in Lucerne
- When: From March 2026
- Where: Lucerne
- Who: Red-green circles
- Amount: 6,300 current hotel beds

In Switzerland, the debate on tourist accommodation has reached a breaking point. While a lack of visitors was once a concern, it is now the excess that is causing dissatisfaction. From Lucerne to Grindelwald, to the small Iseltwald, managing flows on peak days has become an unsustainable challenge.

In Lucerne, tension is palpable. With 1.5 million overnight stays in 2024, the city has set a new historic record, surpassing pre-pandemic volumes. The NZZ newspaper highlighted a symbolic figure: the ratio of tourists to inhabitants in Lucerne is now higher than that of Venice. The political response: an initiative to block new hotels.

Since the end of March 2026, a signature collection has been underway in Lucerne for a popular initiative promoted by red-green circles. The goal is to freeze hotel beds, currently at around 6,300. The proposal provides for an iron rule: the opening of a new facility will only be granted in exchange for the closure of another of the same category. This is not the first time the city has reacted: in 2017, citizens voted to eliminate a bus parking lot in the center, a clear signal against the invasion of tourist buses that had already then raised the issue of overtourism.

### Impact on the real estate market

One of the most critical issues is the impact of tourism on the real estate market. In iconic destinations like Zermatt or St. Moritz, to counteract the phenomenon, several cities have introduced restrictions on short-term rentals: Ticino: rental limit set at a maximum of 90 days per year. Zurich: the municipal council recently supported a similar proposal. Lucerne and Geneva: implementation of regulations to limit platforms such as Airbnb. A study by Switzerland Tourism confirms that, although the sector is perceived as positive for the economy, residents suffer from rising prices, overcrowding of transport, and pollution.

### Grindelwald: an amusement park

At the foot of Eiger, Mönch, and Jungfrau, the historic destination of Grindelwald lives a deep contradiction. Overnight stays have exploded, rising from 1.1 million in 2012 to over 1.6 million today. This success, however, has brought traffic and congestion, pushing many residents to feel like extras in a theme park.

### Iseltwald: the selfie tourism phenomenon

The phenomenon of 'selfie tourism' has also hit tiny and unprepared places. Iseltwald, on Lake Brienz, has become a worldwide pilgrimage site after the success of a South Korean series on Netflix: today, taking a photo on the famous pier costs 5 francs. Overtourism is having a significant impact on the quality of life for residents in Switzerland, particularly in the most popular tourist areas such as Lucerne and Grindelwald. The rise in property prices and short-term rentals is making it difficult for locals to find affordable housing. Additionally, overcrowding on public transport and roads is causing congestion and daily discomfort.

### Restrictions on Airbnb

Restrictions on Airbnb and other short-term rental platforms are a direct response to the increase in property prices and the scarcity of housing for residents. In Ticino, the rental limit set at 90 days per year is a measure aimed at reducing the impact of tourism on the local real estate market. Zurich, Lucerne, and Geneva have followed suit, implementing similar regulations to limit the use of these platforms.

### Environmental Impact

The increase in tourism also has a significant impact on the environment. Overcrowding on public transport and roads is causing an increase in pollution and congestion. Furthermore, the rise in the number of tourists is putting a strain on the natural resources of tourist areas, such as water and energy.

### Local Community Reactions

The reactions of the local community to overtourism are varied. Some residents are concerned about the impact on their lifestyle and quality of life. Others see tourism as an economic opportunity and are in favor of measures to better manage tourist flows. However, there is a growing consensus that a balance needs to be found between tourism and the quality of life for residents.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers living and working in Switzerland, overtourism can have a significant impact on their daily lives. The increase in property prices and short-term rentals can make it difficult to find affordable housing. Additionally, overcrowding on public transport and roads can cause discomfort and delays in daily commutes.

### What to do

If you are a cross-border worker living in Switzerland, there are some measures you can take to mitigate the impact of overtourism on your daily life. First, try to plan your trips in advance to avoid peak hours and periods of high tourist influx. Additionally, consider using alternative means of transport, such as bicycles or less crowded public transport.

### Useful tools

To help you manage your daily life in Switzerland better, you can use tools like the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) and the rental comparator. These tools can help you find affordable housing and plan your expenses better.

### Conclusion

Overtourism is a complex problem that requires careful management and collaboration between local authorities, residents, and tourists. For cross-border workers, it is important to be aware of the impact of tourism on their daily lives and take measures to mitigate these effects. By using tools like the cost of living calculator and the rental comparator, you can find practical solutions to live better in Switzerland.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/turismo/overtourism-svizzera-lucerna-grindelwald-crisi-alloggi/)*

item.title: Varese Hospital: Closures for May 1st and San Vittore
item.description: Changes to healthcare service hours for the May 1st bank holiday and San Vittore's patronal feast in Varese.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Closure of the Ospedale di Circolo Collection Point on May 2
- General closure on May 8 for San Vittore
- Suspended services: CUP, collections, retrieval of reports, administrative offices

## Key facts
- **What**: Changes to healthcare service hours
- When: May 2 and 8, 2026
- Where: Ospedale di Circolo di Varese and ASST Sette Laghi territorial sites
- Who: ASST Sette Laghi
- Amount: Not specified

On the occasion of the upcoming spring bank holiday and the feast of San Vittore, the patron saint of Varese, ASST Sette Laghi has announced some changes to the opening hours of collection points and administrative offices. The week of changes to healthcare service hours in the area will begin on Saturday, May 2, with the closure of the Collection Point at Varese's Ospedale di Circolo, located in the Santa Maria Pavilion. The service will resume regularly the following week.

### General closure for San Vittore

The most significant closure will concern Friday, May 8, the day on which San Vittore, the patron saint of Varese, is celebrated. On this date, the closure will be general and will affect all company sites, from Luino to Tradate. The services suspended for the entire Friday include:
- CUP and Outpatient Clinic Secretariats
- Collection Points and report retrieval desks
- Administrative Reception and URP
- District Offices and territorial sites

The company specifies that the shutdown on May 8 involves the entire territorial network of ASST and not just the facilities in the provincial capital. As always in these cases, emergencies and ward activities remain guaranteed.

### Implications for patients

The closures scheduled for May 2 and 8 may cause inconvenience to patients who need blood tests or to collect reports. It is advisable to book any tests in advance or contact your doctor to check the availability of alternative services. For emergencies, emergency services and ward activities remain operational. ## Analysis of Closures and Alternative Services

The closures scheduled for the May 1st bank holiday and for the San Vittore holiday could have a significant impact on patients who need healthcare services. In particular, the closure of the Blood Drawing Center at the Circolo Hospital in Varese on May 2nd could cause inconvenience to those who need blood tests or to deliver samples. It is important to plan ahead and, if possible, book the tests on the days before or after.

### Alternative Services

For those who need to pick up test results or access administrative services, it is possible to contact their doctor or the clinic's office to check the availability of alternative services. Additionally, you can use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book tests or access test results.

### Comparisons with Other Holidays

The closures scheduled for May 2nd and 8th follow a similar pattern to other national and local holidays. Generally, during these days, non-essential healthcare services are suspended to ensure the rest of the staff and the management of emergencies. However, it is important to note that the closures on May 2nd only affect the Blood Drawing Center of the Circolo Hospital, while those on May 8th are generalized and affect all company locations.

### Practical Scenarios

For patients who need blood tests on May 2nd, it is advisable to book the tests on the days before or after. For those who need to pick up test results or access administrative services, it is possible to contact their doctor or the clinic's office to check the availability of alternative services. Additionally, you can use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book tests or access test results.

### Conclusion

The closures scheduled for the May 1st bank holiday and for the San Vittore holiday could cause inconvenience to patients, but with good planning, it is possible to minimize the inconveniences. It is important to contact your doctor or the clinic's office to check the availability of alternative services and use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book tests or access test results.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do in case of closures

In case of closures of healthcare services, it is important to follow some procedures to minimize inconveniences. Here's what to do:

### Book exams in advance

If you need blood tests or to deliver samples, it is advisable to book the exams on the days before or after the closure. Contact your doctor or the clinic's reception to check the availability of alternative services.

### Use online services

ASST Sette Laghi offers a series of online services that can be used to book exams or access reports. Visit the ASST Sette Laghi website for more information on the available services.

### Contact your doctor

If you need to collect reports or access administrative services, contact your doctor or the clinic's reception to check the availability of alternative services. Your doctor can provide you with information on the available services and how to access them.

### Use emergency services

In case of emergency, emergency room services and ward activities remain operational. If you need immediate assistance, go to the nearest emergency room or contact the emergency number.

### Plan ahead

To avoid inconveniences, it is important to plan ahead. Check the dates of the closures and organize your exams and visits so as not to be caught off guard. Contact your doctor or the clinic's reception to check the availability of alternative services and use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book exams or access reports.

### Conclusion

The closures scheduled for the May 1st bank holiday and for the San Vittore holiday may cause inconveniences to patients, but with good planning, it is possible to minimize the inconveniences. Follow the indicated procedures and use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book exams or access reports. In case of emergency, emergency room services and ward activities remain operational.
